title: Chaenorhinum granatense
taxonomy: Chaenorhinum granatense belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Chaenorhinum, which is a group of small, annual flowering plants. Chaenorhinum granatense is native to the southwestern part of Europe and North Africa. The species is classified within the kingdom Plantae, and its taxonomic hierarchy is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Lamiales, family Scrophulariaceae, genus Chaenorhinum, species C. granatense. Further research may be required to confirm the accuracy of this classification.
uses: Chaenorhinum granatense has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential anti-inflammatory properties. It is sometimes employed to treat various conditions, including fever and digestive issues. However, further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety. The plant's medicinal uses are still being studied and documented. More studies are required to fully understand its potential health benefits.

constituents: Chaenorhinum granatense contains active constituents such as diterpene alkaloids, including chaenorhinine and isochaenorhinine, which have been studied for their potential biological activities.
